tagline: A cosmic encyclopedia where gods, kings, and rituals shape the universe.
what_it_is: The Agni Purana is a vast medieval Sanskrit text (over 15,000 verses) that blends mythology, temple architecture, statecraft, medicine, and yoga into a single encyclopedic work. It is one of the eighteen Mahapuranas, traditionally recited by the fire god Agni to the sage Vasishta, and covers everything from the Ramayana to gemology.
why_it_matters: It offers a rare window into how ancient Indians integrated practical knowledge—like building a palace or treating snakebite—with spiritual cosmology. For a modern reader, it reveals a worldview where no subject is too mundane for divine attention, challenging our separation of science, art, and religion.

famous_stories.title: The Descent of the Ganges
famous_stories.description: King Bhagiratha’s relentless penance brings the celestial river to Earth, but Shiva must catch her in his hair to prevent the world from shattering.

famous_stories.title: Rama’s Coronation and the War with Ravana
famous_stories.description: A condensed Ramayana where Agni himself testifies to Sita’s purity, and Rama’s rule is justified by cosmic law.

famous_stories.title: The Churning of the Ocean
famous_stories.description: Gods and demons churn the Milky Ocean for nectar, unleashing poison, the moon, and the goddess Lakshmi—but Vishnu’s Mohini avatar tricks the demons.

famous_stories.title: The Story of King Shibi
famous_stories.description: King Shibi offers his own flesh to save a dove from a hawk, only to discover the hawk is Indra and the dove is Agni testing his compassion.

famous_stories.title: The Construction of the First Temple
famous_stories.description: Agni instructs Vasishta on the precise measurements for a temple to Vishnu, linking architecture to the body of the cosmic man.

famous_stories.title: The Battle of the Gods and Asuras
famous_stories.description: Agni, as the mouth of the gods, consumes offerings to empower them against the demons, but the asura Taraka learns to counter with his own fire.

entry_chapters.label: Cosmic Origins and the Fire God’s Role
entry_chapters.for_reader: lovers of mythology who want the big creation story

entry_chapters.label: Temple Architecture and Iconography
entry_chapters.for_reader: artists, architects, or anyone curious about sacred geometry

entry_chapters.label: Yoga and Liberation (Moksha)
entry_chapters.for_reader: practitioners of yoga or seekers of spiritual discipline

one_line_pitch: A fire-god’s manual for building a world.
